ontology.foodex2_description: Agar jelly dessert — mapped under 'Gelled desserts and puddings' in FoodEx2 hierarchy
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Agar-Agar Gudir; A0CKP (gelled desserts) is the closest defensible anchor.

allergens: coconut (tree nut — check local regulatory classification)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Agar-Agar Gudir is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one or two small jelly pieces as commonly served at Malay festive gatherings or as an afternoon snack.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Food Composition Database — Kuih and Traditional Malay Desserts; supplemented by HPB Healthhub Nutrition Information and USDA FoodData Central (Agar, dried, SR Legacy ID 11082) for micronutrient cross-reference
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Agar-Agar Gudir as a composite dish. Macronutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore food composition data for traditional Malay agar-based jelly desserts with coconut milk, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for agar (ID 11082) and coconut milk (ID 169919). Input values from enriched_json (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.9) are retained where consistent with HPB/USDA reference ranges. Saturated fat estimated from coconut milk contribution. Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat, cholesterol, and water data not available in primary sources for this composite preparation.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values — Atkinson et al. 2008; analogous estimate based on agar jelly with sugar and coconut milk
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Agar-Agar Gudir in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on agar-based jelly desserts with added sugar (sucrose GI ~65) moderated by agar dietary fibre (viscous fibre effect) and coconut milk fat, consistent with published GI values for similar Southeast Asian agar jellies in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (55 × 11.2) / 100 = 6.16, rounded to 6.2 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (45 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Agar-Agar Gudir is low in sodium at 45 mg per 100 g and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, provided portion sizes are controlled and no additional salt is added during preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (10.5 g per 100 g) with moderate GI of 55
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI is estimated at 55 (low-moderate) due to agar fibre moderating glucose absorption, the high sugar content of 10.5 g per 100 g warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; substituting refined sugar with a low-GI sweetener is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (estimated 3.9 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The coconut milk (santan) component contributes approximately 3.9 g saturated fat per 100 g, which may be a concern for individuals with cardiovascular disease; reduced-fat coconut milk or smaller portions are recommended to limit saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Agar-Agar Gudir is a traditional Malay jelly dessert made primarily from agar-agar powder derived from red algae (Gracilaria or Gelidium species), sugar, and coconut milk (santan). The dessert is prepared by dissolving agar-agar powder in boiling water, combining it with sugar and coconut milk, optionally flavouring with pandan extract or rose syrup, and then chilling the mixture in moulds until set. The result is a firm, smooth, gelatinous confection that is served cold. It is commonly presented in multi-layered or decoratively shaped forms, with colours ranging from white (plain coconut milk layer) to green (pandan) or pink (rose syrup). Nutritionally, a 100 g serving provides approximately 85 kcal, 11.2 g carbohydrate (of which 10.5 g is sugar), 4.5 g fat (predominantly saturated fat from coconut milk), 0.2 g protein, and 1.2 g dietary fibre. The agar-agar base contributes soluble fibre, which moderates the glycemic response despite the added sugar, yielding an estimated GI of 55. Sodium is low at 45 mg per 100 g. Clinically, the high sugar content warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, and the saturated fat from coconut milk is a consideration for cardiovascular health. The dessert is naturally gluten-free and halal. It is a popular treat at Malay weddings, Hari Raya celebrations, and as a refreshing afternoon snack in Malaysia and Singapore.

Is Agar-Agar Gudir suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Agar-Agar Gudir as a named composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore food composition data for traditional Malay agar-based jelly desserts and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for agar and coconut milk. Input values from the enriched_json (ai_generated, confidence 0.9) are consistent with these reference ranges and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.9 g per 100 g) is estimated from the coconut milk (santan) contribution based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy coconut milk data (ID 169919); this value is not directly reported in the input record. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile and moisture data for this traditional composite Malay agar jelly preparation are not available in HPB Singapore food composition database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/absent): agar-agar and coconut milk do not contribute meaningful amounts of these vitamins; values consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy agar (ID 11082) and coconut milk (ID 169919). 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Agar-Agar Gudir exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The food is classified as not fully vegan because coconut milk sourcing practices vary; it is vegetarian. 6. Protein_g (0.2 g) and fat_g (4.5 g) are non-zero and sourced from agar-agar and coconut milk respectively; carbohydrate_g (11.2 g) is non-zero from added sugar. All core macros are nutritionally plausible for this preparation.
